author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter I. 6–14.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is fitting, brethren, that as far as possible we should treat of the text of Holy Scripture, and especially of the Holy Gospel, without omitting any portion, that both we ourselves may derive nourishment according to our capacity, and may minister to you from that source from which we have been nourished.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Last Lord’s day, we remember, we treated of the first section; that is, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same was in the beginning with God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All things were made by Him; and without Him was nothing made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That which was made, in Him is life; and the life was the light of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the light shineth in darkness; and the darkness comprehended it not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So far, I believe, had I advanced in the treatment of the passage: let all who were present recall what was then said; and those of you who were not present, believe me and those who chose to be present.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now therefore,—because we cannot always be repeating everything, out of justice to those who desire to hear what follows, and because repetition of the former thought is a burden to them and deprives them of what succeeds,—let those who were absent on the former occasion refrain from demanding repetition, but, together with those who were here, listen to the present exposition.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It goes on, “There was a man sent from God whose name was John.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Truly, brethren beloved, those things which were said before, were said regarding the ineffable divinity of Christ, and almost ineffably.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who shall comprehend “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And do not allow the name word to appear mean to you, through the habit of daily words, for it is added, “and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This Word is He of whom yesterday we spoke much; and I trust that God was present, and that even from only thus much speaking something reached your hearts.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He is the same, and is in the same manner; as He is, so He is always; He cannot be changed; that is, He is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This His name He spoke to His servant Moses: “I am that I am; and He that is hath sent me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then shall comprehend this when you see that all mortal things are variable; when you see that not only do bodies vary as to their qualities, by being born, by increasing, by becoming less, by dying, but that even souls themselves through the effect of divers volitions are distended and divided; when you see that men can obtain wisdom if they apply themselves to its light and heat, and also lose wisdom if they remove themselves from it through some evil influence?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, you see that all those things are variable, what is that which is, unless that which transcends all things which are so that they are not?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who then can receive this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or who, in what manner soever he may have applied the strength of his mind to touch that which is, can reach to that which he may in any way have touched with his mind?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is as if one were to see his native land at a distance, and the sea intervening; he sees whither he would go, but he has not the means of going.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So we desire to arrive at that our stability where that which is, is, because this alone always is as it is: the sea of this world interrupts our course, even although already we see whither we go; for many do not even see whither they go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That there might be a way by which we could go, He has come from Him to whom we wished to go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what has He done?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has appointed a tree by which we may cross the sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no one is able to cross the sea of this world, unless borne by the cross of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even he who is of weak eyesight sometimes embraces this cross; and he who does not see from afar whither he goes, let him not depart from it, and it will carry him over.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, my brethren, I would desire to have impressed this upon your hearts: if you wish to live in a pious and Christian manner, cling to Christ according to that which He became for us, that you may arrive at Him according to that which is, and according to that which was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He approached, that for us He might become this; because He became that for us, on which the weak may be borne, and cross the sea of this world and reach their native country; where there will be no need of a ship, for no sea is crossed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is better then not to see with the mind that which is, and yet not to depart from the cross of Christ, than to see it with the mind, and despise the cross of Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is good beyond this, and best of all, if it be possible, that we both see whither we ought to go, and hold fast that which carries us as we go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This they were able to do, the great minds of the mountains, who have been called mountains, whom the light of divine justice pre-eminently illuminates; they were able to do this, and saw that which is.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For John seeing said, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw this, and in order that they might arrive at that which they saw from afar, they did not depart from the cross of Christ, and did not despise Christ’s lowliness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But little ones who cannot understand this, who do not depart from the cross and passion and resurrection of Christ, are conducted in that same ship to that which they do not see, in which they also arrive who do see.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But truly there have been some philosophers of this world who have sought for the Creator by means of the creature; for He can be found by means of the creature, as the apostle plainly says, “For the invisible things of Him from the creation of the world are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made, even His eternal power and glory; so they are without excuse.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it follows, “Because that, when they knew God;” he did not say, Because they did not know, but “Because that, when they knew God, they glorified Him not as God, neither were thankful; but became vain in their imaginations, and their foolish heart was darkened.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How darkened?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It follows, when he says more plainly: “Professing themselves to be wise, they became fools.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They saw whither they must come; but ungrateful to Him who afforded them what they saw, they wished to ascribe to themselves what they saw; and having become proud, they lost what they saw, and were turned from it to idols and images, and to the worship of demons, to adore the creature and to despise the Creator.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But these having been blinded did those things, and became proud, that they might be blinded: when they were proud they said that they were wise.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those, therefore, concerning whom he said, “Who, when they had known God,” saw this which John says, that by the Word of God all things were made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these things are also found in the books of the philosophers: and that God has an only-begotten Son, by whom are all things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They were able to see that which is, but they saw it from afar: they were unwilling to hold the lowliness of Christ, in which ship they might have arrived in safety at that which they were able to see from afar and the cross of Christ appeared vile to them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The sea has to be crossed, and dost thou despise the wood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Oh, proud wisdom! thou laughest to scorn the crucified Christ; it is He whom thou dost see from afar: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But wherefore was He crucified?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the wood of His humiliation was needful to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou hadst become swollen with pride, and hadst been cast out far from that fatherland; and by the waves of this world has the way been intercepted, and there is no means of passing to the fatherland unless borne by the wood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ungrateful one! thou laughest Him to scorn who has come to thee that thou mayest return: He has become the way, and that through the sea: thence He walked in the sea to show that there is a way in the sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou who art not able in any way thyself to walk in the sea, be carried in a ship, be carried by the wood: believe in the crucified One, and thou shalt arrive thither.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: On account of thee He was crucified, to teach thee humility; and because if He should come as God, He would not be recognized.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He should come as God, He would not come to those who were not able to see God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For not according to His Godhead does He either come or depart; since He is everywhere present, and is contained in no place.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, according to what did He come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He appeared as a man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, because He was so man, that the God lay hid in Him, there was sent before Him a great man, by whose testimony He might be found to be more than man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was a man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how could that man speak the truth concerning God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was sent by God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was he called?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Whose name was John.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore did he come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He came for a witness, that he might bear witness concerning the light, that all might believe through him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of man was he who was to bear witness concerning the light?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Something great was that John, vast merit, great grace, great loftiness!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Admire, by all means, admire; but as it were a mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But a mountain is in darkness unless it be clothed with light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore only admire John that you may hear what follows, “He was not that light;” lest if, when thou thinkest the mountain to be the light, thou make shipwreck on the mountain, and find not consolation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what oughtest thou to admire?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The mountain as a mountain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lift thyself up to Him who illuminates the mountain, which for this end was elevated that it might be the first to receive the rays, and make them known to your eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, “he was not that light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore then did he come?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But that he might bear witness concerning the light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That all might believe through him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And concerning what light was he to bear witness?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That was the true light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore is it added true?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because an enlightened man is also called a light; but the true light is that which enlightens.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even our eyes are called lights; and nevertheless, unless either during the night a lamp is lighted, or during the day the sun goes forth, these lights are open in vain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, therefore, John was a light, but not the true light; because, if not enlightened, he would have been darkness; but, by enlightenment, he became a light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For unless he had been enlightened he would have been darkness, as all those once impious men, to whom, as believers, the apostle said, “Ye were sometimes darkness.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, because they had believed, what?—“but now are ye light,” he says, “in the Lord.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Unless he had added “in the Lord,” we should not have understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Light,” he says, “in the Lord:” darkness you were not in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “For ye were sometimes darkness,” where he did not add in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore, darkness in you, light in the Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And thus “he was not that light, but was sent to bear witness of the light.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But where is that light?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was the true light, which lighteth every man that cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If every man that cometh, then also John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The true light, therefore, enlightened him by whom He desired Himself to be pointed out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Understand, beloved, for He came to infirm minds, to wounded hearts, to the gaze of dim-eyed souls.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this purpose had He come.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence was the soul able to see that which perfectly is?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Even as it commonly happens, that by means of some illuminated body, the sun, which we cannot see with the eyes, is known to have arisen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because even those who have wounded eyes are able to see a wall illuminated and enlightened by the sun, or a mountain, or a tree, or anything of that sort; and, by means of another body illuminated, that arising is shown to those who are not as yet able to gaze on it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus, therefore all those to whom Christ came were not fit to see Him: upon John He shed the beams of His light; and by means of him confessing himself to have been irradiated and enlightened, not claiming to be one who irradiates and enlightens, He is known who enlightens, He is known who illuminates, He is known who fills.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And who is it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He who lighteth every man,” he says, “who cometh into the world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if man had not receded from that light, he would not have required to be illuminated; but for this reason has he to be illuminated here, because he departed from that light by which man might always have been illuminated.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If He came hither, where was He?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was in this world.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was both here and came hither; He was here according to His divinity, and He came hither according to the flesh; because when He was here according to His divinity, He could not be seen by the foolish, by the blind, and the wicked.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These wicked men are the darkness concerning which it was said, “The light shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehended it not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Behold, both here He is now, and here He was, and here He is always; and He never departs, departs no-whither.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is need that thou have some means whereby thou mayest see that which never departs from thee; there is need that thou depart not from Him who departs no-whither; there is need that thou desert not, and thou shalt not be deserted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Do not fall, and His sun will not set to thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou fallest, His sun setteth upon thee; but if thou standest, He is present with thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But thou hast not stood: remember how thou hast fallen, how he who fell before thee cast thee down.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he cast thee down, not by violence, not by assault, but by thine own will.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For hadst thou not consented unto evil, thou wouldest have stood, thou wouldest have remained enlightened.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But now, because thou hast already fallen, and hast become wounded in heart,—the organ by which that light can be seen,—He came to thee such as thou mightest see; and He in such fashion manifested Himself as man, that He sought testimony from man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From man God seeks testimony, and God has man as a witness;—God has man as a witness, but on account of man: so infirm are we.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By a lamp we seek the day; because John himself was called a lamp, the Lord saying, “He was a burning and a shining light; and ye were willing for a season to rejoice in his light: but I have greater witness than John.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore He showed that for the sake of men He desired to have Himself revealed by a lamp to the faith of those who believed, that by means of the same lamp His enemies might be confounded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There were enemies who tempted Him, and said, “Tell us by what authority doest thou these things?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I also,” saith He, “will ask you one question; answer me.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The baptism of John, whence was it? from heaven, or of men?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they were troubled, and said among themselves, If we shall say, From heaven, he will say unto us, Why did ye not believe him?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: (Because he had borne testimony to Christ, and had said, I am not the Christ, but He.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “But if we shall say, Of men, we fear the people, lest they should stone us: for they held John as a prophet.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Afraid of stoning, but fearing more to confess the truth, they answered a lie to the Truth; and “wickedness imposed a lie upon itself.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they said, “We know not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord, because they shut the door against themselves, by professing ignorance of what they knew, did not open to them, because they did not knock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is said, “Knock, and it shall be opened unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not only did these not knock that it might be opened to them; but, by denying that they knew, they barred that door against themselves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the Lord says to them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And they were confounded by means of John; and in them were the words fulfilled, “I have ordained a lamp for mine anointed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His enemies will I clothe with shame.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He was in the world, and the world was made by Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Think not that He was in the world as the earth is in the world, as the sky is in the world, as the sun is in the world, the moon and the stars, trees, cattle, and men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was not thus in the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in what manner then?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As the Artificer governing what He had made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He did not make it as a carpenter makes a chest.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The chest which he makes is outside the carpenter, and so it is put in another place, while being made; and although the workman is nigh, he sits in another place, and is external to that which he fashions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But God, infused into the world, fashions it; being everywhere present He fashions, and withdraweth not Himself elsewhere, nor doth He, as it were, handle from without, the matter which He fashions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By the presence of His majesty He maketh what He maketh; His presence governs what He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore was He in the world as the Maker of the world; for, “The world was made by Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What meaneth “the world was made by Him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The heaven, the earth, the sea, and all things which are therein, are called the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Again, in another signification, those who love the world are called the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The world was made by Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did not the heavens know their Creator, or did the angels not know their Creator, or did the stars not know their Creator, whom the demons confess?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All things from all sides gave testimony.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who did not know?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who, for their love of the world, are called the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By loving we dwell with the heart; but because of their loving the world they deserved to be called after the name of that in which they dwelt.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In the same manner as we say, This house is bad, or this house is good, we do not in calling the one bad or the other good accuse or praise the walls; but by a bad house we mean a house with bad inhabitants, and by a good house, a house with good inhabitants.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner we call those the world who by loving it, inhabit the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those who love the world; for they dwell with their hearts in the world.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For those who do not love the world in the flesh, indeed, sojourn in the world, but in their hearts they dwell in heaven, as the apostle says, “Our conversation is in heaven.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “the world was made by Him, and the world knew Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He came unto His own,”—because all these things were made by Him,—“and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who are they?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The men whom He made.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Jews whom He at the first made to be above all nations.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because other nations worshipped idols and served demons; but that people was born of the seed of Abraham, and in an eminent sense His own, because kindred through that flesh which He deigned to assume.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “He came unto His own, and His own received Him not.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did they not receive Him at all? did no one receive Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Was there no one saved?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For no one shall be saved unless he who shall have received the coming Christ.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But John adds: “As many as received Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What did He afford to them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great benevolence!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Great mercy!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was born the only Son of God, and was unwilling to remain alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Many men, when they have not sons, in advanced age adopt a son, and thus obtain by an exercise of will what nature has denied to them: this men do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if any one have an only son, he rejoices the more in him; because he alone will possess everything, and he will not have any one to divide with him the inheritance, so that he should be poorer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not so God: that same only Son whom He had begotten, and by whom He created all things, He sent into this world that He might not be alone, but might have adopted brethren.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For we were not born of God in the manner in which the Only-begotten was born of Him, but were adopted by His grace.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He, the Only-begotten, came to loose the sins in which we were entangled, and whose burden hindered our adoption: those whom He wished to make brethren to Himself, He Himself loosed, and made joint-heirs.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For so saith the apostle, “But if a son, then an heir through God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And again, “Heirs of God, and joint-heirs with Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He did not fear to have joint-heirs, because His heritage does not become narrow if many are possessors.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Those very persons, He being possessor, become His inheritance, and He in turn becomes their inheritance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear in what manner they become His inheritance: “The Lord hath said unto me, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten Thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Ask of me, and I will give Thee the nations for Thine inheritance.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear in what manner He becomes their inheritance.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He says in the Psalms: “The Lord is the portion of mine inheritance, and of my cup.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us possess Him, and let Him possess us: let Him possess us as Lord; let us possess Him as salvation, let us possess Him as light.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then did He give to them who received Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “To them He gave power to become sons of God, even to them that believe on His name;” that they may cling to the wood and cross the sea.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how are they born?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because they become sons of God and brethren of Christ, they are certainly born.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if they are not born, how can they be sons?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the sons of men are born of flesh and blood, and of the will of man, and of the embrace of wedlock.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But in what manner are they born?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Who not of bloods,” as if of male and female.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Bloods is not Latin; but because it is plural in Greek, the interpreter preferred so to express it, and to speak bad Latin according to the grammarian that he might make the matter plain to the understanding of the weak among his hearers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if he had said blood in the singular number, he would not have explained what he desired; for men are born of the bloods of male and female.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us say so, then, and not fear the ferule of grammarians, so long as we reach the solid and certain truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who understands it and blames it, is thankless for his having understood.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Not of bloods, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The apostle puts flesh for woman; because, when she was made of his rib, Adam said, “This is now bone of my bone, and flesh of my flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And the apostle saith, “He that loveth his wife loveth himself; for no one ever hated his own flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Flesh, then, is put for woman, in the same manner that spirit is sometimes put for husband.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because the one rules, the other is ruled; the one ought to command, the other to serve.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For where the flesh commands and the spirit serves, the house is turned the wrong way.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What can be worse than a house where the woman has the mastery over the man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that house is rightly ordered where the man commands and the woman obeys.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In like manner that man is rightly ordered where the spirit commands and the flesh serves.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: These, then, “were born not of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But that men might be born of God, God was first born of them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Christ is God, and Christ was born of men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It was only a mother, indeed, that He sought upon earth; because He had already a Father in heaven: He by whom we were to be created was born of God, and He by whom we were to be re-created was born of a woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Marvel not, then, O man, that thou art made a son by grace, that thou art born of God according to His Word.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Word Himself first chose to be born of man, that thou mightest be born of God unto salvation, and say to thyself, Not without reason did God wish to be born of man, but because He counted me of some importance, that He might make me immortal, and for me be born as a mortal man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When, therefore, he had said, “born of God,” lest we should, as it were, be filled with amazement and trembling at such grace, at grace so great as to exceed belief that men are born of God, as if assuring thee, he says, “And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, dost thou marvel that men are born of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider God Himself born of men: “And the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But because “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us,” by His very nativity he made an eye-salve to cleanse the eyes of our heart, and to enable us to see His majesty by means of His humility.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore “the Word was made flesh, and dwelt among us:” He healed our eyes; and what follows?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And we beheld His glory.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His glory can no one see unless healed by the humility of His flesh.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore were we not able to see?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Consider, then, dearly beloved, and see what I say.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There had dashed into man’s eye, as it were, dust, earth; it had wounded the eye, and it could not see the light: that wounded eye is anointed; by earth it was wounded, and earth is applied to it for healing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all eye-salves and medicines are derived from the earth alone.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By dust thou wert blinded, and by dust thou art healed: flesh, then, had wounded thee, flesh heals thee.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul had become carnal by consenting to the affections of the flesh; thus had the eye of the heart been blinded.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The Word was made flesh:” that Physician made for thee an eye-salve.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And as He thus came by flesh to extinguish the vices of the flesh, and by death to slay death; therefore did this take place in thee, that, as “the Word became flesh,” thou mayest be able to say, “And we beheld His glory.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What sort of glory?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such as He became as Son of man?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That was His humility, not His glory.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But to what is the sight of man brought when cured by means of flesh?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “We beheld His glory, the glory as of the Only-begotten from the Father, full of grace and truth.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Of grace and truth we shall speak more fully in another place in this same Gospel, if the Lord vouchsafe us opportunity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let these things suffice for the present, and be ye edified in Christ: be ye comforted in faith, and watch in good works, and see that ye do not depart from the wood by which ye may cross the sea.
